note: the test body asserts nothing at all -- passes regardless of what compute() does.
the only call-under-test is wrapped in a broad no-op except, with no assertion elsewhere.
test_inner is test-shaped and has its own assertion, but pytest's collector never descends into a function body looking for further defs -- it can never be independently run, skipped, or reported.
the skipif condition is a literal tautology (True) -- the test can never actually run.

source:
def test_a():
    x = 1
    y = 2
    assert x == y
note: comparing two DIFFERENT expressions is a real equality check, not a tautology (assert x == x).

superficially resembles the swallowed_failure shape (a try/except around a call), but the except is NARROW (ValueError, not broad/no-op-Exception) and the call chains to a same-file helper that itself asserts -- a legitimate narrow-except-around-a-real-check pattern, not hollow.
a hollow-shaped (no_assertion) function, but the file doesn't match pytest's test-file discovery convention (test_*.py / *_test.py), so it is never scanned at all.
